parentQuestion: Which part of the cerebral cortex is involved in intellect, cognition, recall, and personality?

scaffolds.q: Different parts of the cortex have different functions (localization).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Occipital = vision, temporal = hearing/memory, parietal = sensation, frontal = motor/executive.

scaffolds.q: The frontal lobe is involved in planning, decision-making, and personality.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The frontal lobe is the 'executive' of the brain - planning, judgment, impulse control.

scaffolds.q: The PREFRONTAL cortex is the front part of the frontal lobe.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Pre- (in front of) frontal. It's the most anterior part of the frontal lobe.

scaffolds.q: The prefrontal cortex handles 'executive functions' - like a CEO of the brain.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Working memory, planning, judgment, impulse control, personality, abstract reasoning.

scaffolds.q: Phineas Gage's injury to prefrontal cortex changed his personality dramatically.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Responsible foreman became impulsive and unreliable after tamping iron destroyed his PFC.

scaffolds.q: The prefrontal cortex is the LAST brain region to fully myelinate (~age 25).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! This explains adolescent impulsivity - the brake pedal isn't fully connected yet.

scaffolds.q: The 'anterior association area' is another name for the prefrontal cortex.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Anterior (front) association cortex. It integrates information from everywhere to guide behavior.

scaffolds.q: Intellect + cognition + recall + personality = prefrontal cortex functions.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The prefrontal cortex is the seat of executive function - planning, working memory, personality.

scaffolds.q: The answer is PREFRONTAL CORTEX (anterior association area).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not posterior association (sensory integration), not limbic (emotion/memory encoding), not somatosensory (body sensation). Prefrontal cortex = executive function, personality, higher cognition.
